message:
feat(wasm-abi): implement memory ownership protocol for JS<->WASM boundary (asupersync-umelq.8.2)

Implement strict memory ownership model for handles crossing the JS<->WASM
boundary, preventing leaks and use-after-free at the API level:

- WasmHandleOwnership: three-state ownership tracking (WasmOwned,
  TransferredToJs, Released) for all boundary handles
- WasmHandleTable: slot-based allocator with generation counters for
  stale-handle rejection, LIFO free-list recycling, pin/unpin protocol,
  ownership transfer, and leak detection diagnostics
- WasmHandleEntry: per-handle metadata combining boundary state,
  ownership, and pin status
- WasmBufferTransfer/WasmBufferTransferMode: explicit buffer transfer
  semantics (Copy vs Transfer) for large data payloads
- WasmHandleLifecycleEvent/WasmHandleEventKind: structured events for
  deterministic observability of handle lifecycle transitions
- WasmMemoryReport: diagnostic report with handle counts by kind/state,
  pin status, and capacity metrics
- WasmHandleError: comprehensive error types for all ownership violations

16 new deterministic tests covering:
- Allocation, lookup, and slot recycling with generation bumps
- Full handle lifecycle (Unbound->Bound->Active->Cancel->Drain->Close)
- Stale handle rejection (use-after-free prevention)
- Pin/unpin protocol with release guards
- Ownership transfer to JS with double-transfer prevention
- Leak detection for closed-but-unreleased handles
- Memory report accuracy
- Cancellation+pinning integration flow
- Serialization round-trips for all new types

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
